name: Odesa (Ukraine)

colors.symbolism: the historic field of the city arms

description: A red field charged with the coat of arms of Odesa — a golden anchor on a red shield within a wreath — alluding to the city's role as Ukraine's principal Black Sea port.
history: Odesa, founded in 1794 by decree of Catherine II, has been Ukraine's principal Black Sea port and one of its largest cities since the 19th century. The anchor in the city arms refers to this maritime role.

features.symbolism: the city's identity as Ukraine's principal Black Sea port
